examples: His discovery, the "daguerreotype," was announced in 1839.
Photography did indeed make certain kinds of painting obsolete — the daguerreotype virtually did away with the portrait miniature.
The daguerreotype was a unique image that reproduced what was in front of the camera lens in minute, unselective detail and could not be duplicated.
Her aim was to mimic the daguerreotype process used back then, limiting herself to one frame per sitter.
Inside, the house is a faded daguerreotype of wood-panelled walls, brown linoleum floors and a cast-iron kitchen stove.
